message:
feat(cass/index): NDJSON progress events on stderr for json/robot mode, and status-missing-db clarifies as not-initialized

Adds an opt-out stream of NDJSON progress events that the `cass index`
command emits on stderr when `--json` / `--robot` is active, so long-running
indexing operations are observable without having to scrape stdout or
poll the filesystem.

Changes:

* `src/indexer/mod.rs`
    - `IndexingProgress::phase_label()` — human label ("preparing" / "scanning"
      / "indexing") derived from the atomic `phase` counter.
    - `IndexingProgress::snapshot_json(elapsed_ms)` — captures a
      `serde_json::Value` holding phase, total, current, discovered_agents,
      is_rebuilding, agent_names, and elapsed_ms. Shaped for direct emission
      as an NDJSON line.

* `src/lib.rs`
    - New `--progress-interval-ms <ms>` arg on the `index` command (default
      2000, clamped to [250, 60000] downstream), and `--no-progress-events`
      to fully disable the stream. Env-var override
      `CASS_INDEX_NO_PROGRESS_EVENTS=1` honored for caller scripts that
      want to suppress without changing flags.
    - In `--json` / `--robot` mode, the command spawns a throttle loop that
      calls `snapshot_json(elapsed)` at the configured interval and writes
      each snapshot as a single NDJSON line to stderr — preserving the
      contract that stdout carries only the final tool result.

* `tests/cli_robot.rs`
    - Renames `status_missing_db_reports_not_found` →
      `status_missing_db_reports_not_initialized` and updates the assertion
      to match the clarified `status.json` output: a brand-new data directory
      now surfaces `"not_initialized"` with an initialization-guidance hint,
      instead of the older "not_found" which was ambiguous with "db file
      was deleted after initialization". The test content expands to lock
      down the new output shape.

* `tests/fixtures/cli_contract/introspect.json`
    - Re-snapshot of the introspect-contract fixture to include the two new
      `index` args (`--progress-interval-ms`, `--no-progress-events`) and
      the refined status output shape.

Existing callers that do not pass the new flags get the default 2 s cadence,
which is quiet enough for short indexes (the throttle elides events that
would fire less than the interval apart) and responsive enough for large
imports. Existing tools that scrape stdout are unaffected — the events are
on stderr by design.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
